mergeRationale: The patch only rejects the literal domain string '__proto__'; the index is still a plain object, so __proto__ at the path/key layer and other magic keys still reach Object.prototype.

requiredFindings.summary: The patch adds a string blacklist for one domain value while the domain/path/key index stays an ordinary object.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: __proto__ used as the path or key (this.idx[domain]['__proto__'], this.idx[domain][path]['__proto__']) still hits the prototype chain, other magic keys like constructor/prototype are not blocked, and any variant that parses back to __proto__ bypasses an exact-string check. Only a data-structure fix closes the class.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Use Object.create(null) or Map for every index layer (and/or reject unsafe keys centrally), then re-run the PoC plus variant and nested-key tests.

requiredFindings.summary: The new test only replays __proto__ at the domain layer.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Prototype-pollution defenses need variant and nested-layer tests: __proto__ as path and key, constructor/prototype keys, and a check that the index has no prototype.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Add tests for __proto__ as path and key, constructor/prototype keys, and confirm the index carries no prototype.

optionalFindings.summary: An exact-string equality check is bypassed by any case or encoding variant that resolves back to __proto__ elsewhere.

disallowedConclusions: The patch can be merged because the __proto__ PoC test passes.
Rejecting the __proto__ domain is enough because that is the string in the published PoC.
